channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Federal judge allows Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to continue
item.description: U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper decided he could not grant a motion from unions representing the workers to temporarily block President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers. He did, however, acknowledge the chaos caused by Trump’s blitz of second-term executive actions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spring break warning: Most unlikely American tourist hot spots emerge as deadly risk
item.description: The Caribbean, a popular spring break destination for Americans, is relatively safe, but the State Department is warning U.S. tourists to stay away from certain countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyers to deliver closing arguments in the trial of man charged with stabbing Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Lawyers are set to deliver their closing arguments Friday in the trial of a New Jersey man charged with trying to kill Salman Rushdie on a New York lecture stage in a knife attack that left the author blind in one eye and with other serious injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione set for first court appearance since his arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare in New York City and leading authorities on a five-day manhunt is scheduled to be in court Friday for the first time since his December arraignment on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Netflix to invest $1 billion on Mexican films and series
item.description: Netflix has pledged $1 billion to develop and produce films and series in Mexico over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Rep. Burchett: Congress to blame if Trump agenda fails
item.description: GOP Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett tells NewsNation he knows whose fault it will be if Donald Trump's agenda doesn't succeed.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KTLA 5

item.title: Huge cuts in National Institutes of Health research funding go before a federal judge
item.description: A court battle is set to resume over the Trump administration’s drastic cuts in medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione case, from UnitedHealthcare CEO killing to court dates
item.description: According to police, Luigi Mangione planned this shooting and came from Pennsylvania to make it happen.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: 23% of Americans ended a relationship over money: Survey
item.description: A new report shows nearly 1 in 4 Americans have ended a relationship because they weren't on the same page when it comes to finances.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts event honoring Black History Month
item.description: The celebration comes amid the administration's moves to gut federal diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs and place DEI employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justin Bieber 'depressed' after alleged exploitation by friends: Exclusive
item.description: Justin Bieber is facing one of the most difficult times of his life, according to sources close with him.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Woman injured after ice slides off roof of tractor-trailer
item.description: Dan DeCesare said his wife, who was in the passenger seat, was hit directly in the face and chest by the chunk of ice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration nixes legal representation for thousands of migrant children
item.description: President Trump has made cracking down on immigration a key tenet of his second term, including in some cases the elimination of certain government services.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Kohl's to close 27 underperforming stores by April 2025
item.description: Kohl's has announced the closure of 27 stores and the San Bernardino E-commerce Distribution center due to underperformance and the need to focus on long-term growth and health of the business.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen Curry captures MVP honors in All-Star Game at home, leads Shaq’s OGs to victory
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The final pregame introduction belonged to none other than Stephen Curry. The big MVP trophy at the end, Curry heaved that up into the air, too, in a fitting finish to his All-Star Game at home. His arena. His fans.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire. And in the end, Boston's Jayson Tatum had the dunk that decided a crown.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend, as recently hired employees who review the safety of food ingredients, medical devices and other products were fired.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, ABC 4
